2605.07823.title: SCENE: Recognizing Social Norms and Sanctioning in Group Chats
2605.07823.paper_summary: Online group chats are social spaces with implicit behavior patterns that, when broken, are often met with social sanctioning from the group. The ability and willingness of LLM-based agents to recognize and adapt to these norms remains mostly unexplored. We introduce SCENE, a social-interaction benchmark focused on implicit norms and social sanctioning in multi-party chat. SCENE generates plausible non-roleplay scenarios with scripted personas that follow a hidden norm, create opportunities for the subject agent to violate it, and sanction breaches when they occur. We further propose behavioral evaluation metrics for two functional adaptation abilities: responsiveness to negative sanctioning, and adapting norm from peers behavior. We evaluate six frontier and open-weight models on SCENE. Our results show that Claude Opus 4.7 and Gemini 3.1 Pro adapt to implicit norms significantly more than the evaluated open-weight models. SCENE contributes one benchmark in the direction of recent calls for dynamic, interactional evaluation of LLM social capabilities.
2605.07823.paper_summary_zh: 線上群組聊天是具有隱含行為模式的社交空間，當這些模式被打破時，通常會受到群體的社會制裁。基於LLM的代理人識別和適應這些規範的能力和意願仍然大多未被探索。我們介紹了SCENE，一個專注於多方聊天中的隱含規範和社會制裁的社交互動基準。SCENE生成合理的非角色扮演場景，並使用遵循隱藏規範的劇本角色，創造讓主體代理人違反規範的機會，並在違規時進行制裁。我們進一步提出了兩種功能適應能力的行為評估指標：對負面制裁的反應能力，以及從同儕行為中適應規範的能力。我們在SCENE上評估了六個前沿和開放權重模型。我們的結果顯示，Claude Opus 4.7和Gemini 3.1 Pro對隱含規範的適應性顯著高於被評估的開放權重模型。SCENE為最近對LLM社交能力的動態互動評估的呼籲貢獻了一個基準。
2605.07823.author: Mateusz Jacniacki et.al.
